header.location: Pittsburgh, PA (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Strategic Program Manager who thrives on translating data into insights to ensure continuous program improvement and strong stakeholder collaboration. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve student and program experience—through human-centered, data-driven approaches.

bullets.text: Manage 20+ educational programs, 10+ vendors, and program budgets, refining operations and communications strategy via feedback analysis
bullets.original: Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders to identify learning gaps and design 3 new educational programs
bullets.reason: Lead bullet swapped to program operations + communications — matches Columbia's mandate to 'oversee day-to-day administration' and 'manage communications, including program newsletters'

bullets.text: Facilitate listening sessions with 25+ {cross|stakeholders} (students, counselors, partners), resulting in 3 new programs aligned with {ex|student needs}
bullets.original: Develop guides and workflows for 70+ cross-functional staff, improving program knowledge and reducing onboarding time by 75%
bullets.reason: Surfaces student + counselor coordination — direct InGenius-to-Columbia bridge for 'primary administrative liaison between students, DUS, and university offices'

bullets.text: Establish {coord|cross-functional processes} and documentation for 70+ staff, streamlining program operations and reducing onboarding time by 75%

bullets.reason: Process documentation + supervisory adjacency — supports 'supervise and coordinate workflow of the Undergraduate Program Coordinator' and registrar/admin office coordination

bullets.text: Facilitate {prog|webinars and info sessions} for 600+ student and parent participants, driving a 78% {prog|program enrollment rate}

bullets.reason: Reworded 'training webinars/participants/client conversion' → 'webinars and info sessions/student and parent participants/program enrollment' — mirrors Columbia's 'workshops, advising initiatives, informational sessions' and outreach to current/prospective majors

bullets.text: Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into {data|actionable reports} that informed engagement strategies

bullets.reason: Surfaced 'survey responses' to directly map JD's 'surveys related to program quality and student experience' + 'analyze survey results and prepare summary reports'

jd.meta: Columbia University, INCITE/Trust Collaboratory & Center for Smart Streetscapes — Community Outreach Program Coordinator · New York, NY · $66,300–$67,300 · Officer of Administration, Grade 10 · 1-year contract (renewable) · Posted Apr 25, 2026 · ⚠️ Internal candidate identified

sections.title: Role Overview
sections.content: Reports to the Director of Academic Administration and Finance. Oversees {coord|day-to-day administration and coordination} of the Department's undergraduate program. Manages {prog|program operations}, {coord|student advising coordination}, {prog|communications}, and {prog|academic initiatives} that support {ex|student success and program quality}. Works closely with faculty, students, and university offices.

items.text: Serve as {cross|primary administrative liaison} between students, DUS, Columbia College, GS, and the Registrar's Office
Coordinate with advising offices across the University (Berick Center, GS Advising, CCE, Global Engagement)
Manage {prog|faculty advisor assignment} process and Graduate Student Advisors Program
Supervise and coordinate workflow of the Undergraduate Program Coordinator
Partner with faculty committees to implement {prog|departmental policies and procedures}
Coordinate course scheduling, curricular planning, and {coord|TA assignments} with MA/PhD Program Managers
Compile and analyze {data|program data} to prepare {data|reports} for faculty leadership and committees
Develop and coordinate {prog|workshops, advising initiatives, informational sessions} for academic enrichment
Organize departmental events and {prog|outreach for prospective majors and current students}
Manage communications: {prog|newsletters, announcements}, program policies, deadlines, opportunities
Coordinate undergraduate {prog|honors, prizes, and Wueller Teaching Awards} process
Coordinate {data|program assessment}, {data|student exit interviews and surveys} on program quality and experience
Analyze survey results and prepare {data|summary reports} for faculty leadership to inform improvements
Bachelor's degree or equivalent ✅ (BA Psychology & Education Studies + MS Organizational Psychology)
Minimum 1-3 years of relevant experience ✅ (~3 years OD/PM/HR)
Preferred: Prior experience in academic environment ⚠️ (InGenius Prep is adjacent — higher-ed counseling, not university administration)
Preferred: Knowledge of university academic systems ⚠️ (limited direct university admin exposure)
Proficiency with Microsoft Office and Google Drive ✅

sections.title: 🏛️ H1B Status
sections.content: Columbia University = accredited non-profit university → CAP-EXEMPT. No H1B lottery risk. Cap-exempt status is the fastest sponsorship lane during Jamie's runway window — applications submittable year-round, processing typically 2-4 months.

changeLog.change: Per Jamie's feedback: identity changed to 'Strategic Program Manager' (dropped OD framing). Removed 'across stakeholders', 'educational', 'actionable', and 'and teams' to bring summary to 3 lines. Core phrases now: coordinating programs / translating program data into improvements / communicating with students.

Per Jamie: dropped ODN (HR consulting on absenteeism is methodologically transferable but framing too distant from undergraduate program administration). Added EXPLO Curriculum Developer & Course Instructor (June–Aug 2019) back to surface curriculum design + instructional materials credential. All 5 remaining roles are now education/student-facing. The diagnostic data analysis ODN was carrying is already covered by NextGen 2,000+ survey work, Wesleyan SA MaxQda focus groups, and InGenius feedback analysis.

changeLog.section: InGenius — Set B (Program Management/Engagement) with student-facing tilt
changeLog.change: Changed InGenius title to 'Educational Program Manager' (direct higher-ed bridge, cleaner than 'Program Manager, Educational Programs'). Lead bullet: program operations + communications strategy. Bullet 2: listening sessions with students/counselors/partners. Bullet 3: cross-functional processes (process documentation + supervisory adjacency). Bullet 4: webinars/info sessions for student and parent participants → program enrollment rate.
Bullet 2 changed 'employee experiences' to 'survey responses' to directly mirror JD's 'analyze survey results and prepare summary reports' language.
Moved HR newsletter + well-being initiatives bullet to lead — direct bridge to JD's 'program newsletters and announcements' + 'Wueller Teaching Awards process' (recognition coordination).

changeLog.section: Kronos replaced with Wesleyan Study Abroad Ambassador
changeLog.change: Per Jamie: removed Kronos (HR internship) and added Wesleyan University — Fries Center for Global Studies, Study Abroad Ambassador (Aug–Dec 2021, part-time). Three bullets surface (1) coordinating annual International Education Week with school offices + student orgs, (2) MaxQda qualitative focus-group analysis on study abroad barriers, (3) globally-focused event programming for community building. Initially considered EXPLO (curriculum/teaching) but Wesleyan SA is the LITERAL match — it's university-side administration + event coordination + focus group assessment, all at a university, which is exactly Columbia's setting.

changeLog.section: Projects — Elanco out, College Admissions Counseling in
changeLog.change: Per Jamie's request: removed Elanco employer-branding line (employer-branding consulting is not relevant to undergrad program management) and added College Admissions Counseling (Part-Time): 4 students over 2 years through career exploration, college list development, essay writing — direct student-advising credential.
Added Education Studies coursework (Educational Psychology, Practicum in Education Studies, Learning and Motivation, Psychological Testing) — material credential for higher-ed program manager role.
Per memory rule: 'Organization Development Network (ODN) Oregon' (full form, never 'Transition Projects Inc.').

Led with Project Management (Asana, Airtable, Notion) + Google Workspace + MS Office — directly mirrors JD's 'Proficiency with Microsoft Office and Google Drive.' Preserved Data Analysis and LMS for higher-ed context. Dropped HRCI cert (not relevant for academic admin).
